description.en: The `/etc/gshadow-` file is used to store backup information about groups that is critical to the security of those accounts, such as the hashed password and other security information.

rationale.en: It is critical to ensure that the `/etc/gshadow-` file is protected from unauthorized access. Although it is protected by default, the file permissions could be changed either inadvertently or through malicious actions.

audit.en:
Run **one** of the following commands to set ownership of `/etc/gshadow-` to `root` and group to either `root` or `shadow`:  

Run the following command to remove excess permissions form `/etc/gshadow-`:

```
# chmod u-x,g-wx,o-rwx /etc/gshadow-
```

```
# chown root:shadow /etc/gshadow-
  -OR-
# chown root:root /etc/gshadow-
```

remediation.en:
Run **one** of the following commands to set ownership of `/etc/gshadow-` to `root` and group to either `root` or `shadow`:  

Run the following command to remove excess permissions form `/etc/gshadow-`:

```
# chmod u-x,g-wx,o-rwx /etc/gshadow-
```

```
# chown root:shadow /etc/gshadow-
  -OR-
# chown root:root /etc/gshadow-
```
